title: asplenium trichomanes subsp. trichomanes
intro: Asplenium trichomanes subsp. trichomanes, also known as maidenhair spleenwort, is a type of fern that belongs to the Aspleniaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of maidenhair spleenwort include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as having antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, maidenhair spleenwort is often used as a dried herb or made into a salve, and can be combined with other herbs to create teas and infusions for various health benefits.
